item.title: FSN E-Commerce Ventures Ltd Falls 4.29%: Key Technical and Valuation Shifts This Week
item.description: FSN E-Commerce Ventures Ltd experienced a challenging week, closing at Rs.254.10 on 6 March 2026, down 4.29% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.265.50. This decline slightly outpaced the Sensex’s 3.00% fall over the same period, reflecting a mix of technical momentum shifts, valuation recalibrations, and market volatility that influenced investor sentiment throughout the week. Time: 1 minute ago

item.title: One 97 Communications Ltd Falls 5.25%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: One 97 Communications Ltd experienced a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining 5.25% to close at Rs.1,038.55, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by significant intraday lows, a gap down opening amid market concerns, and a technical momentum shift signalling increased caution among investors. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: Sapphire Foods India Ltd Falls 5.97%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Sapphire Foods India Ltd closed the week ending 6 March 2026 at Rs.192.85, down 5.97% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.205.10. This decline outpaced the Sensex’s 3.00% fall over the same period, reflecting intensified bearish momentum amid technical downgrades and volatile trading sessions. Despite a strong gap up on the final trading day, the stock struggled to sustain gains, underscoring ongoing challenges in regaining upward momentum. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: Tarsons Products Ltd Dips 0.55%: 3 Key Factors Behind the Volatile Week
item.description: Tarsons Products Ltd’s stock experienced a volatile week ending 6 March 2026, closing marginally lower by 0.55% at Rs.189.35, while the Sensex declined more sharply by 3.00%. The stock showed sharp intraday swings, hitting an all-time low of Rs.176.05 early in the week before rebounding midweek with a 6.06% surge. Despite this, persistent financial headwinds and valuation concerns kept investor sentiment cautious throughout the period. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: Latent View Analytics Ltd Falls 9.68%: 5 Key Factors Behind the Steep Decline
item.description: Latent View Analytics Ltd’s stock endured a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, declining 9.68% to close at Rs.304.80, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% drop. The stock hit multiple 52-week lows and an all-time low amid a combination of technical weakness, rating downgrades, and broader market volatility, despite the company’s steady profit growth and low debt profile. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: Go Fashion Falls 5.02% This Week: Key Financial Strains and Market Pressures
item.description: Go Fashion (India) Ltd’s shares declined by 5.02% over the week ending 6 March 2026, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00%. The stock hit a new all-time low of Rs.280.55 on 2 March amid disappointing quarterly results and sustained sectoral pressures. Despite some pockets of operational strength, the company’s financial metrics and technical indicators signal ongoing challenges. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: Tega Industries Ltd Falls 4.21%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Tega Industries Ltd closed the week ending 6 March 2026 at Rs.1,743.40, down 4.21% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.1,820.10. This decline slightly underperformed the Sensex’s 3.00% fall over the same period, reflecting a week marked by significant volatility, a sharp downgrade to a Strong Sell rating, and deteriorating technical momentum. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: Quarterly Earnings Review: Dec-2025 Results Reveal Mixed Trends Across Market Caps
item.description: The December 2025 quarter earnings season has concluded for 4,093 stocks, revealing a nuanced picture of corporate performance across market capitalisations and sectors. While the proportion of companies reporting positive results has inched up slightly to 46.0%, the data underscores ongoing challenges amid a complex macroeconomic backdrop. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Supriya Lifescience Falls 4.07%: Technical Downgrade and Valuation Concerns Weigh
item.description: Supriya Lifescience Ltd experienced a challenging week on the bourses, closing down 4.07% from ₹653.35 to ₹626.75, marginally underperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by a significant downgrade to a Sell rating amid deteriorating technical indicators and valuation concerns, which weighed heavily on investor sentiment and contributed to the stock’s downward trajectory. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: AGS Transact Technologies Ltd Drops 7.65%: 8 Key Factors Behind the Steep Decline
item.description: AGS Transact Technologies Ltd endured a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its share price falling 7.65% to close at ₹3.26, significantly underperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock hit multiple 52-week and all-time lows amid sustained selling pressure, deteriorating financial metrics, and weak technical indicators, reflecting ongoing operational and market headwinds. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: AWL Agri Business Ltd Falls 6.24% Amidst Prolonged Downtrend and Profitability Challenges
item.description: AWL Agri Business Ltd’s shares declined by 6.24% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.177.20 from Rs.189.00 the previous Friday. This underperformance notably exceeded the Sensex’s 3.00% fall during the same period, reflecting persistent negative sentiment amid deteriorating financial results, reduced promoter confidence, and a prolonged downtrend marked by multiple fresh 52-week and all-time lows. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Medplus Health Services Ltd’s 0.73% Weekly Dip Amid Technical and Fundamental Concerns
item.description: Medplus Health Services Ltd closed the week marginally lower by 0.73% at Rs.841.15, underperforming the broader Sensex which declined 3.00%. The week was marked by a significant downgrade to a Sell rating amid deteriorating technical indicators and fundamental concerns, followed by a subtle shift to a mildly bearish technical outlook. Despite these headwinds, the stock showed resilience with gains on the last two trading days, reflecting mixed investor sentiment. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Anand Rathi Wealth Ltd Gains 3.79%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Anand Rathi Wealth Ltd delivered a solid weekly gain of 3.79%, closing at Rs.3,192.00 on 6 March 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock’s resilience amid a broadly negative market was supported by a mix of fundamental reassessments and nuanced technical momentum shifts, reflecting both strength and caution in investor sentiment. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Data Patterns Surges 8.78%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Rally
item.description: Data Patterns (India) Ltd delivered a strong weekly performance, surging 8.78% from Rs.3,208.45 to Rs.3,490.15 between 2 and 6 March 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock hit multiple 52-week highs during the week, supported by robust technical indicators, increased institutional interest, and a recent upgrade to a Hold rating by MarketsMOJO, underscoring renewed investor confidence amid a mixed broader market environment. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Metro Brands Ltd Falls 7.60%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Metro Brands Ltd’s stock declined by 7.60% over the week ending 6 March 2026, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00%. The footwear company faced intensified bearish technical signals, culminating in a fresh 52-week low despite a broadly mixed market backdrop. Key events including a shift to a bearish trend, technical downgrades, and sustained price weakness shaped the week’s performance. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Vedant Fashions Declines 5.81%: 4 Key Factors Behind the Steep Slide
item.description: Vedant Fashions Ltd’s shares declined by 5.81% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.367.25 from Rs.389.90 the previous Friday. This underperformance outpaced the Sensex’s 3.00% fall, reflecting persistent downward pressure on the stock amid weak financial results, valuation concerns, and sustained bearish momentum. Despite brief intraday recoveries, the stock hit multiple 52-week and all-time lows during the week, underscoring ongoing challenges within the garments and apparels sector. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: HP Adhesives Ltd Falls 5.09%: 4 Key Factors Behind the Steep Decline
item.description: HP Adhesives Ltd’s stock declined by 5.09% over the week ending 6 March 2026, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00%. The stock hit fresh 52-week and all-time lows amid continued financial challenges and bearish market sentiment. Despite a modest recovery in the final two trading sessions, the week was marked by sustained downward pressure driven by weak quarterly results, valuation shifts, and persistent underperformance relative to benchmarks. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Gateway Distriparks Declines 3.25%: Mixed Technical Signals Shape Weekly Performance
item.description: Gateway Distriparks Ltd closed the week down 3.25% at Rs.56.93, slightly underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% decline over the same period. The stock faced a challenging week marked by a sharp gap down on 2 March, mixed technical momentum shifts midweek, and a cautious close on 6 March amid volatile market conditions. Despite some short-term bullish signals, the overall sentiment remained subdued, reflecting broader market pressures and sector-specific headwinds. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: GMR Power & Urban Infra Ltd Falls 10.66%: Key Factors Behind the Sharp Weekly Decline
item.description: GMR Power & Urban Infra Ltd experienced a turbulent week ending 6 March 2026, with its share price declining sharply by 10.66% from Rs.120.05 to Rs.107.25. This underperformance contrasted with the broader Sensex, which fell by 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by significant selling pressure, a sharp gap down opening on 2 March, and heightened volatility amid a recent downgrade to a Strong Sell rating by MarketsMOJO. Time: 58 minutes ago

item.title: Krishival Foods Gains 0.72%: 3 Key Technical Shifts Shape Weekly Performance
item.description: Krishival Foods Ltd closed the week with a modest gain of 0.72%, ending at Rs.314.50 on 6 March 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock’s price action was marked by notable technical developments, including an upgrade to a Hold rating, a shift to a sideways momentum, and a subsequent mild bearish correction. These events collectively shaped a week of cautious optimism amid broader market volatility. Time: 58 minutes ago
